recent_calls.summary: The user reported that their AC is not working and requested urgent service due to high temperatures. The agent documented and escalated the request as urgent but informed the user that service would begin at 8 AM due to business hours. The user ultimately declined further contact, stating they would handle the issue themselves.

The user, Ron Winchester, requested a callback regarding a service issue with his cooling system and refrigerant charge. The agent confirmed the details, including the service address and contact information, and scheduled a callback for a regular service appointment. The user declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

Linda called to report that after a recent thermostat replacement, her air conditioning is not cooling and the temperature inside her home remains high, which is an emergency due to her asthma. The agent documented the issue, confirmed her address and contact details, and escalated the case for priority follow-up on the next business day. The user was informed that a team member will contact her at her work number to resolve the issue.

The user, Hannah, called to request an urgent update on a dispatch due to a child with asthma at the service address. The agent documented the request, confirmed details, and escalated the issue for priority handling on the next business day. The call ended with the user satisfied and no further requests.

Tonya called to check if the technician scheduled to visit her home was still coming today, as he had not arrived yet. The agent informed her that it was after business hours and that a representative would follow up with her on the next business day. Tonya declined further assistance and the call ended with a promise of a callback.

The user reported an HVAC emergency involving no cooling and confirmed it was not related to a gas leak. The agent collected the user's full name, phone number, and service address, then documented and escalated the emergency for follow-up since it was after regular hours.

Ruth from RingCentral called after hours looking to speak with Jacob Kerrks regarding an account check and to address any questions or concerns about their services. The agent informed her that a representative would contact her on the next business day since it was after hours. The call ended politely with no further assistance requested.

Denise McNeil called to discuss ongoing issues with her air conditioner that were not resolved after a previous service visit. The after-hours assistant documented her concerns and informed her that a representative would call her back on the next business day. Denise declined further assistance and ended the call politely.

The user, Anne, called after hours requesting to speak with Nicole about a technician visit. The agent informed her that Nicole was unavailable and documented her request for a callback on the next business day. The call ended with no further assistance requested.

The user called to inquire about the status of a repair estimate for a property at 505 Ridge Drive, Florissant. The agent informed the user that they would pass the request to the team for follow-up on the next business day and updated the user's contact number. The call ended with the user expressing thanks and no further questions.

The user called after hours to report an issue with their air conditioner, which was serviced earlier that day but is still not cooling properly. The user requested a callback to address the problem and asked for the issue to be reported. The agent acknowledged the request and informed the user that the team would follow up on the next business day.

Denise called to inquire about cleaning her malfunctioning air conditioning unit and whether she should contact her warranty provider. The after-hours assistant documented her issue and informed her that a representative would call her back on the next business day to discuss options. Denise declined further assistance and ended the call politely.

The user called Budget's after-hours assistant to report an issue with a furnace humidifier installation at 4164 Gallatin Lane by David Whitfield, stating it was not sealed properly. The user requested a callback at their phone number. The call ended after the user provided their contact information.

Keith called to report that his technician, Devon, did not show up for a scheduled appointment and failed to communicate the absence. He expressed frustration and requested urgent assistance and a callback from a human representative. The agent documented the issue as urgent and informed Keith that a representative would call him back on the next business day.
The call was very brief and ended immediately after the agent introduced herself, with no further interaction from the user.

Anne called after receiving a call from Budget and wanted to speak with someone from the company. The agent informed her that since it was after business hours, a representative would call her back the next business day. Anne did not request any additional assistance.

The user called after hours requesting a callback from the operations manager to discuss matters on behalf of Donovan Smith. The agent documented the request and informed the user that a representative would contact them on the next business day. The call ended with the user declining further assistance.

The user called to inquire about their appointment and expressed urgency, but clarified it was not an emergency. The agent offered to have the team follow up on the next business day to prioritize the request.

Judith called to report an active water leak at her home and confirmed her service address and contact details. The agent documented the urgent issue and escalated it for priority follow-up on the next business day since it was after hours. Judith declined any further assistance and the call ended with the agent assuring a callback.
The user called after hours and requested to speak to the office. The agent informed the user that the office was unavailable and offered to take a message, but the user declined and ended the call.

Heather from Business Funding called to inform the agent about a potential preapproval for a business line of credit and requested a callback with completed application and bank statements. The call ended after Heather provided her contact information and a polite closing.

The user called to confirm if the technician Devon was still coming today. The agent confirmed the appointment scheduled for 2 PM onwards and informed the user about the notification process. The user had no further questions and ended the call.

The user called to clarify a voicemail about an order form and was informed by the agent that they would be transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call ended with a successful transfer to another agent.
The user called and requested to speak to a representative. The agent acknowledged the request and successfully transferred the call to a live agent.

The user called to discuss a recommendation for purchasing a new furnace priced at nine thousand dollars. The initial agent, Sophie, attempted to connect the user with a live agent for further assistance and successfully transferred the call.

The user called to request the installation date and warranty information for an HVAC system installed years ago for her sister. The agent collected all necessary details including the address, names, model and serial numbers, order number, and contact information, and confirmed the preferred follow-up by email. The agent assured the user that the team would provide the installation date, warranty details, and unit brand/type to pass on to the new homeowner.
The user requested to speak with CG, and the agent attempted to connect the user with a live agent named CG by transferring the call successfully.

The user called to inform the agent that they want to cancel the service request because the issue is not with the air conditioning but a roof leak. The user confirmed the cancellation and then ended the call after checking if the agent was still there.

The user Tess from American Home Shield called to discuss a dispatch number related to a work order for Walter Cashion. The agent initially asked for the service address ZIP code, but the call ended abruptly after the user repeatedly tried to get the agent's attention without response.

Dornio, a supervisor at Glen and Bogle Hills in Saint Charles, called to request a representative visit to add Budget as a vendor for heating and cooling emergency services at their commercial property. The agent confirmed the details and scheduled a visit for Tuesday between 10 and 12. Dornio declined information about maintenance programs but provided positive feedback about the call.

The user Sheila from American Home Shield called to check on a service dispatch for Jimmy Coleman and requested to speak with a live representative to know the soonest availability for the customer's location. The agent attempted to assist but then transferred the call to a live agent for further help.
The user requested to speak with a real person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user, Chris Dameron, called to request a plumbing service for water backing up in the bathtub, possibly from the sewage pipe, at their residential property. The agent booked a provisional appointment for July 1st at 8 AM and informed the user about the dispatch fee and confirmation process. The user declined to provide feedback at the end of the call.

Sarah updated the agent about a plumbing leak issue being handled by a warranty company and Action Mechanical, causing a delay in scheduling repairs. She confirmed details for the service address and warranty dispatch number, and expressed interest in scheduling central air and disconnect work after the plumbing repair is completed. The agent noted the information and offered maintenance program details, which Sarah declined, and she provided positive feedback about the call.
The user called Budget and requested to speak with a live representative. The agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent as requested.
The user requested to be transferred to a live representative, and the agent successfully transferred the call to another agent.

Christine LaFavor, a realtor, called to inquire about the ETA of a Budget agent scheduled to visit a house on the Cedars at 1 PM, as an agent has been waiting for almost an hour. The call was transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Brandon Besher, called to inquire about a recent appointment scheduled for today. The agent was unable to find the appointment in the system and offered to transfer the user to a live representative, which the user accepted. The call was successfully transferred to a live agent.

The user initiated a call to inquire about something but hung up abruptly after a brief exchange with the agent. The agent greeted the user and asked how to assist, but the user did not complete their request.
The user called to inquire about an AC appointment scheduled for today but the call ended abruptly before the agent could provide assistance.

The user, Virginia Robinson, called to schedule a prepaid follow-up HVAC appointment to check her air conditioner. The agent confirmed the details and booked the appointment for Friday, July 3rd, from 2 to 4 PM. The user declined to provide feedback and the call ended successfully.
The user called and requested to speak with someone about their account. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user, Betty, expressed frustration and requested to speak with a supervisor regarding issues with her account. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent who could assist further.
The user requested to speak with a service agent named Amy. The agent attempted to transfer the call to Amy and successfully completed the transfer.

The user called to schedule an appointment for an American Home Shield home warranty issue with their cooling system leaking and overflowing. The agent attempted to collect the dispatch number but the user was unable to provide it and requested to speak with a representative. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The user called to discuss a quote about their air conditioner and was transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, facilitated the transfer promptly.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

Jennifer from American Home Shield called to get an update on a dispatch for homeowner Dorothy Harris regarding a compressor part. The agent confirmed the details but could not find the appointment and offered to transfer Jennifer to a live representative, which she accepted. The call was successfully transferred to a live agent for further assistance.
The user mistakenly called Budget Heating, Cooling, and Plumbing thinking it was UnitedHealthcare. After realizing the error, the user ended the call quickly without requesting any services.
